summary: Iter 14 BUG-FIX VERIFICATION (Realtime Chat token-key) — PASSED. Backend pytest: 15/15 green in 13.8s (WS presence on paid booking for both customer & artist, two-client realtime message propagation, bad-token rejection, unpaid 4402 rejection, REST fallback get/post, Iter 11/12/13 endpoint regression). Frontend E2E (Playwright, real browser, REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL): logged in as customer@booktalent.com, opened paid booking BT-260625-99978D '💬 Chat' modal → data-testid='chat-status' flipped from '○ Reconnecting' to '● Live' (class 'pill pill-green') in <1s, '2 online' shown, message send successful with ✓✓ read receipt. Bug fix (ChatBox.jsx line 59: 'token' → 'bt_token') is fully effective.
critical_code_review_comments: ChatBox.jsx WS effect only runs after access?.enabled becomes true; effect dependency array [bookingId, user, access?.enabled] is correct. Token now read from 'bt_token' — matches auth.jsx (key set on login at lines 20/27, cleared at line 33), api.js axios interceptor (line 9), CustomerDashboard.jsx, BookingFlow.jsx — single source of truth confirmed.

Minor UX: chat-status briefly shows '○ Reconnecting' for ~300-500ms while the WebSocket handshake completes. Consider initial state 'Connecting…' (neutral) instead of 'Reconnecting' (suggests failure) to avoid alarming users. Cosmetic only.

Iter 13 noted server.py was still 2,378 lines after the refactor — chat routes (REST + WS) remain in server.py because they share in-memory connection state with the WS endpoint. Future refactor could extract chat domain together with its WS manager.

WebSocket URL construction `${api.defaults.baseURL}/ws/chat/{bid}?token=…` relies on baseURL ending in /api — works correctly (verified WS upgrade 101 against wss://booktalent-audit.preview.emergentagent.com/api/ws/chat/{bid}). Kubernetes ingress preserves the WS Upgrade header for /api/ws/* paths.
Optional hardening: consider passing JWT via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol or a short-lived ticket cookie instead of query-string to avoid token leakage in proxy/access logs.
seed_data_creation: Sent 1 throwaway chat message ('iter14-rest-hello' via REST + 'hello iter14 e2e' via WS) on paid booking cca6a262-…-21dc13d52c7 — visible in chat history, no impact on business flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified end-to-end. /app/backend/tests/test_iter14.py is the new baseline for chat-related regression; reuse it together with test_iter12.py and test_iter13.py. Booking IDs stable: PAID=cca6a262-8393-4970-bd38-021dc13d52c7, UNPAID=060a549a-0952-4425-84c0-422210ee501e. WS URL pattern: wss://<host>/api/ws/chat/{booking_id}?token=<jwt>. Server emits {event:'presence', participants:[...]} as first frame on successful connect. Frontend localStorage key for JWT is 'bt_token' (do NOT use 'token').
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: ChatBox.jsx line 59 was reading the JWT with localStorage.getItem('token'); the rest of the app stores the JWT under localStorage.getItem('bt_token') (set in auth.jsx login, used in api.js axios interceptor). REPRODUCTION (pre-fix): customer login → open paid-booking chat → WS URL becomes /api/ws/chat/{bid}?token=null → backend's jwt_decode raises → server closes with code 4001 (invalid token) → onclose sets connected=false → pill stuck on '○ Reconnecting' forever. REST chat history still loaded fine because axios uses the correct key. MITIGATION: one-line change to localStorage.getItem('bt_token'). VERIFICATION: WS now negotiates 101 Switching Protocols against wss://…/api/ws/chat/{bid}?token=<valid-jwt>, server emits presence frame, ChatBox.onopen sets connected=true, pill flips to green '● Live' (verified visually and via class assertion 'pill pill-green'). Send/receive/typing/presence/read-receipt all functional in the live UI.
